task_completion.description: Navigate to the mid-sagittal plane of the MRHead MRI where the corpus callosum is clearly visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sagittal slice viewer in the medical imaging software displays the characteristic 'C' shape of the corpus callosum, indicating the mid-sagittal plane.

task_completion.description: Measure the total genu-to-splenium length of the corpus callosum.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line markup or ruler tool is placed on the sagittal view, extending from the anterior end (genu) to the posterior end (splenium) of the corpus callosum.

task_completion.description: Measure the thickness of the corpus callosum at the genu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A short line markup or ruler tool is placed across the anterior bulbous part (genu) of the corpus callosum to measure its width/thickness.

task_completion.description: Measure the thickness of the corpus callosum at the body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A short line markup or ruler tool is placed across the middle section (body) of the corpus callosum to measure its width/thickness.

task_completion.description: Measure the thickness of the corpus callosum at the splenium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A short line markup or ruler tool is placed across the posterior bulbous part (splenium) of the corpus callosum to measure its width/thickness.

task_completion.description: Save the measurement markups and the JSON report with all required fields (including atrophy grade).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or save dialog showing the creation of 'cc_measurements.mrk.json' and 'corpus_callosum_report.json'. The report must contain the fields: total_length_mm, genu_thickness_mm, body_thickness_mm, splenium_thickness_mm, mid_sagittal_slice, and atrophy_grade.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used medical imaging software (like 3D Slicer) to view the MRI and place markups.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the MRHead volume loaded in a graphical interface with visual measurement tools actively used, rather than just running a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the values in the JSON report correspond to the actual measurements taken in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values entered into the 'corpus_callosum_report.json' should roughly match the lengths displayed next to the line markups in the imaging software's viewer.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply copy-paste a pre-made JSON file without performing the visual task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be a clear progression of opening the MRI, navigating slices, placing multiple distinct markups, and then compiling those specific results into the final report.
